articles.title: Storms Ending Heat Wave - Tathya
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Andaman and Nicobar Islands
articles.full_text:
Bhubaneswar: Odisha is currently experiencing a transition from intense heat to widespread storm activity as a cyclonic circulation persists over the interior regions.
While some western and interior areas recently saw temperatures reaching between 40°C and 43°C, a cooling trend is underway with day temperatures expected to drop by 3 to 4 degrees Celsius.
The current weather in Odisha is 29°C with a feels-like temperature of 32°C under partly sunny skies. Winds are light at 7 mph from the southeast with humidity at 59% and a high UV index of 9.
Conditions will remain unsettled in the coming days with light rain and thunderstorms expected through May 7. Today, May 5, brings a 50% chance of rain during the day and 65% at night.
Tomorrow, May 6, will be partly sunny with a high of 33°C and a low of 22°C, though rain chances persist at 25-30%. By May 7, light rain is likely again with a 60% chance of precipitation and a high of 32°C.
Looking further ahead, a low pressure system is forecast to develop in the Bay of Bengal around May 11, which may intensify into a cyclonic storm by mid-May, potentially bringing more significant rain and wind to coastal regions.
